name: From the Roman bridge of Villadiego to Olmos de la Picaza — circular through the Villadiego Plateau
name_original: Del puente románico de Villadiego a Olmos de la Picaza — circular por el Páramo de Villadiego
region_intro: Jogging around Villadiego offers a blend of historical charm and natural beauty within the province of Burgos, Spain. The region features diverse landscapes, including river systems, waterfalls, and the expansive Villadiego Plateau, providing varied terrain for running. Trails often incorporate medieval heritage sites, allowing for a combination of physical activity and cultural exploration. The area's natural features create scenic backdrops for running routes. Best jogging routes around Villadiego The most popular jogging route is From the Roman bridge of Villadiego to Olmos de la Picaza — circular through the Villadiego Plateau, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 15 minutes to complete, offering open views across the plateau. Another top favourite among local runners is Medieval Bridge of Villadiego loop from Villadiego, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.1 km) path. This route provides a scenic loop directly from Villadiego, incorporating local historical landmarks. Local runners also love the Cueva de Valdegoba loop from Huérmeces, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 1 hour 29 minutes. Jogging around Villadiego is defined by river paths, open plateau landscapes, and routes that pass through historical areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging trails with elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 16 runners have used komoot to explore Villadiego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Villadiego?
A: There are currently &lt;strong&gt;3&lt;/strong&gt; curated running routes around Villadiego available on komoot, offering a mix of moderate and challenging options. These routes showcase the diverse landscapes of the region, from open plateaus to historical areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Villadiego?
A: Jogging routes in Villadiego feature varied terrain, including open plateau landscapes, river paths, and trails that pass through historical areas. You'll find routes with gentle elevation changes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051959443/from-the-roman-bridge-of-villadiego-to-olmos-de-la-picaza-circular-through-the-villadiego-plateau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Roman bridge of Villadiego to Olmos de la Picaza — circular through the Villadiego Plateau&lt;/a&gt;, and more challenging options with significant climbs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31575551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Valdegoba loop from Huérmeces&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Villadiego area?
A: Yes, several running routes around Villadiego are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051959443/from-the-roman-bridge-of-villadiego-to-olmos-de-la-picaza-circular-through-the-villadiego-plateau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Roman bridge of Villadiego to Olmos de la Picaza — circular through the Villadiego Plateau&lt;/a&gt;, which offers open views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Villadiego loop from Villadiego&lt;/a&gt;, which incorporates local historical landmarks.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Villadiego?
A: The Villadiego region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can encounter picturesque waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cañada Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4382544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona&lt;/a&gt;, which feeds the Rudrón river. For those seeking expansive views, the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Amaya&lt;/a&gt; offers a rewarding challenge. Many routes also pass through areas with medieval heritage, allowing you to combine exercise with cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any challenging trail running options near Villadiego?
A: For runners looking for a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31575551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Valdegoba loop from Huérmeces&lt;/a&gt; is classified as a difficult route. It covers 7.0 miles (11.3 km) and features significant elevation gain, making it ideal for those who enjoy more strenuous trail running.

Q: What do other runners say about the routes in Villadiego?
A: More than 16 runners have used komoot to explore Villadiego's varied terrain. They often praise the blend of historical charm and natural beauty, highlighting the scenic backdrops provided by river systems, open plateaus, and historical sites. The routes offer options for different ability levels, ensuring an enjoyable experience for many.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging routes in Villadiego?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, routes with moderate difficulty and relatively flat terrain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Villadiego loop from Villadiego&lt;/a&gt;, could be suitable for families. These paths often follow river systems or open plateaus, providing accessible and enjoyable environments for a run or walk together.

Q: Can I find routes that are suitable for beginners or easy jogging?
A: Yes, there are moderate routes that are well-suited for beginners or those looking for an easier jog. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Villadiego loop from Villadiego&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.6 miles (9.1 km) path that provides a scenic loop directly from Villadiego, incorporating local historical landmarks without extreme elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Villadiego?
A: The region of Burgos, where Villadiego is located, experiences diverse seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for running, with lush greenery in spring and beautiful foliage in autumn. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, while winters can be cold. Checking local weather forecasts before your visit is always recommended.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails around Villadiego?
A: Many natural trails and paths in the Villadiego area are generally dog-friendly, especially those through open plateaus and along river systems. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially near livestock or in protected natural areas, and to respect local regulations regarding pets. Always carry water for your dog, especially on longer runs.

Q: Are there places to park near the running routes in Villadiego?
A: Villadiego, being a smaller town, typically offers accessible parking options, especially near its main attractions or town center. For routes starting directly from Villadiego, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Villadiego loop from Villadiego&lt;/a&gt;, you can usually find parking within the town. For routes starting from nearby villages like Huérmeces, local parking is often available.
tips: In and around Olmos de la Picaza, you can enjoy outdoor activities such as hiking and cycling. In addition, the region has several points of historical and cultural interest, such as churches and traditional buildings that reflect the typical architecture of the area.

The Villadiego bridge is an example of civil architecture that is made up of three arches, the central one being higher and wider than the side ones. It is made of stone, its construction is ashlar and it has a vaulted structure.

waypoints.name: Medieval bridge over the Brullés River (Villadiego)
